title: The Resurgence Of The “New Three”
gist: China's "new three" export categories—electric vehicles, lithium-ion batteries and solar cells—remain the highest-profile flashpoints of trade tension over China's manufacturing capacity, with Western partners alleging domestic overcapacity is pushing these goods onto global markets.

insights.claim: In 2023 China's government coined the label "new three" for three fast-growing export products—electric vehicles, lithium-ion batteries and solar cells—that symbolized the success of its industrial policy and move up the value chain.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: These three categories are the key barometers to watch for China-West trade friction and industrial-policy signaling.
insights.quote: electric vehicles, lithium-ion batteries and solar cells
insights.locator: In 2023, China’s government applied that label

insights.claim: China's trading partners allege the rapid rise in exports of EVs, batteries and solar cells was pushed onto global markets by domestic excess capacity rather than pulled by genuine global demand.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: The overcapacity-versus-demand debate frames the tariff and trade-defense risk facing exporters in these sectors.
insights.quote: pushed onto global markets by domestic excess capacity, rather than being pulled by global demand for high-quality goods
insights.locator: Exports of these products rose so rapidly

insights.claim: On a 2024 trip to China, then-US Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen warned that China's overcapacity in the "new three" was too large for the rest of the world to absorb.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: High-level US pushback signals continued policy pressure and potential trade barriers against Chinese EV, battery and solar exports.
insights.quote: simply too large for the rest of the world to absorb
insights.locator: On a trip to China in 2024

insights.claim: China's state media rejected the overcapacity charge, attributing rapid export gains to rising global demand and the efficiency of Chinese enterprises.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Beijing's framing implies it will resist concessions on export volumes, keeping trade tensions elevated.
insights.quote: equating more export products to excess capacity is completely unfounded

insights.claim: Exports of the "new three" had already plateaued after their 2021-22 surge by the time of Yellen's 2024 overcapacity comments.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: The plateau suggests the overcapacity alarm may lag the actual export cycle—watch for whether a resurgence reignites the dispute.
insights.quote: exports of the new three had already plateaued after their 2021-22 surge
insights.locator: Yet at the time of Yellen’s comments
